The question is asking to determine the first five-year interval where the deaths per year exceeded 100,000. To find this, we need to sort the data based on the "deaths per year" column in the order of "large to small" using the f_sort_column(deaths per year) operation. This will allow us to see the intervals with the highest death rates first and identify the first one that exceeds 100,000 deaths per year.

The question is asking to identify the first five-year interval where the number of deaths exceeded 100,000. Each row represents a time period with corresponding death statistics in the "deaths per year" column. To find the answer, we can examine each row to locate the first interval where the "deaths per year" value surpasses 100,000. We don't need to group the data in this case since we are comparing individual values directly.

The first interval with more than 100,000 deaths was 1965-1970, which had 105,000 deaths per year. Prior to this, the 1960-1965 period had 89,000 deaths, which is below 100,000. Therefore, the first interval to exceed 100,000 deaths per year was 1965-1970.

judge_thought:
Explanation: The question asks for the first five-year interval where the number of deaths exceeded 100,000. Looking at the "deaths per year" column in the original table:
1950-1955: 66,000 (below 100,000)
1955-1960: 76,000 (below 100,000)
1960-1965: 89,000 (below 100,000)
1965-1970: 105,000 (first interval above 100,000)
1970-1975: 121,000 (above 100,000, but not the first)
Thus, the first five-year interval to have more than 100,000 deaths is 1965-1970, making the Prediction Answer correct.
